_readme: Copy to $COLLAB_HOME/seats.json and edit. A seat with backend 'cli' is automated: its 'cmd' is run with the prompt on STDIN and the reply read from STDOUT (no shell). The driver prepends 'system' into the prompt, so a backend only needs its bare read-stdin/print-stdout mode. A seat WITHOUT a cli backend (omit it, or backend != 'cli') is the human/web seat: the driver leaves its handoffs for the Telegram bridge. 'timeout' is per-invocation seconds. Exact argv is tool/version specific; the contract is: read prompt on stdin, write reply on stdout, exit 0.

builder.system: You are the BUILDER in a collab-kit handoff loop. Read the reviewer's handoff below and respond with your implementation notes / decisions. Do not restate the whole design; answer the reviewer's points.

reviewer.system: You are the independent REVIEWER in a collab-kit handoff loop. Be skeptical and specific: verify claims against the described behavior, name concrete blockers with failing scenarios, and do not sign off unless it is genuinely production-grade.

_other_backends_you_could_use.note: The adapter (tools/adapters/openai-compatible-seat.py) reads keys from .env and speaks any OpenAI-shaped API. Swap --base/--model/--key-env per provider. Use --api responses for OpenAI models that reject /chat/completions (gpt-5-codex etc.); leave it off (auto) and it falls back on the 404 anyway.
_other_backends_you_could_use.any_stdin_cli: Any command that reads the prompt on stdin and prints the reply on stdout drops in with zero code changes ([C34]) — the adapter is just the version-proof option for HTTP APIs.

_web_seat_example.note: A non-cli seat is NOT automated. Its handoffs are left in place so the Telegram bridge delivers them to a human (or a browser agent) who replies with /c <project> <message>. Use this for the seat you want to keep a human/web agent on.
